title: phaca stenophylla
intro: Phaca stenophylla, also known as the dwarf lupine, is a perennial flowering plant native to the alpine regions of the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Phaca stenophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Phaca stenophylla can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
